title: 10 best herbal teas for runny nose
intro_desc: Herbal teas for runny nose are natural remedies that help alleviate congestion and nasal discharge by promoting decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The best herbal teas for runny nose include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Sambucus nigra, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Rosmarinus officinalis, Echinacea angustifolia, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, runny nose can disrupt daily life, causing fatigue, decreased productivity, and social isolation, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural remedy that can help with a runny nose. This is because eucalyptus oil, which is found in the leaves of the eucalyptus tree, has decongestant properties. When you drink eucalyptus globulus tea, the eucalyptus oil is released into your body and helps to thin and clear out mucus from your nose and sinuses. This can provide quick relief from congestion and a runny nose, making it easier to breathe and feel better. Eucalyptus globulus tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, further helping to alleviate a runny nose. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tea is a natural expectorant, which means it can help to loosen and bring up mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and clear out.

Echinacea purpurea tea has been used for a long time to help with colds and runny noses. It contains special compounds that boost the immune system, which helps the body fight off infections that can cause a runny nose. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages. This makes it easier to breathe and feel better. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea tea also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. When taken as a tea, Echinacea purpurea can help soothe a sore throat and calm a cough, which can be helpful when you have a runny nose. Some people also use it to help prevent illnesses from occurring in the first place, by giving their immune system a boost.

Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy that can help with a runny nose. The main reason is that ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This makes it easier for you to breathe and feel better. The tea also contains compounds that help to thin out mucus, making it less likely to clog your nose. When you drink ginger tea, the warm liquid helps to loosen up any blockages in your sinuses, allowing you to breathe more easily. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger also help to reduce congestion and sinus pressure, giving you relief from a runny nose.

Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a herb that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues, including a runny nose. The reason thyme tea is good for a runny nose is that it has natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. These properties help to fight off infections that cause a runny nose, such as the common cold or flu. Thyme tea also contains compounds that help to thin and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe. The essential oils in thyme, particularly thymol, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses. When you drink thyme tea, the compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to calm down your nasal passages and sinuses, reducing congestion and runny nose symptoms. Thyme tea is also easy to make and can be enjoyed hot or cold, making it a convenient remedy for a runny nose.

Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for a runny nose. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain special chemicals that help reduce swelling in the nasal passages. When the nasal passages swell, they produce extra mucus, causing a runny nose. The chemicals in Ginkgo biloba tea calm down the swelling, allowing the nasal passages to return to normal. This helps to reduce the amount of mucus produced, making it easier to breathe and feel better. Ginkgo biloba tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and irritation in the nasal passages. As a result, drinking Ginkgo biloba tea may help to alleviate symptoms of a runny nose, such as congestion and sinus pressure. By reducing inflammation and swelling, Ginkgo biloba tea may also help to prevent sinus infections and other complications.

Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary tea, is a herbal remedy that can help alleviate a runny nose. The tea is made from the leaves of the rosemary plant, which have natural decongestant properties. When you drink rosemary tea, the decongestant compounds can help to open up airways and reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This can make it easier to breathe and can help to stop the flow of mucus, which can be causing your runny nose. Additionally, rosemary tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the nasal lining and reduce irritation. As a result, rosemary tea may help to provide relief from congestion and sinus pressure, making it a popular choice for people who suffer from allergies or colds.

Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a runny nose. This is because the tea contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink the tea, the glycyrrhizin travels to your nose and reduces swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages. As a result, the production of mucus in your nose decreases, and you experience less congestion and dripping. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce the irritation and redness in the nasal area, making it a good option for people with allergies or sinusitis. The tea may also help to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel from your body.

Sambucus nigra tea is a natural remedy that can help with a runny nose. This is because it contains a type of plant compound called flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink Sambucus nigra tea, these compounds go into your body and help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in your nose. This can make it easier to breathe and help to dry up a runny nose. Additionally, Sambucus nigra tea also contains a type of plant compound called vitamin C, which is known for its immune-boosting properties. This can help to fight off underlying infections that may be causing your runny nose in the first place. By combining these two types of benefits, Sambucus nigra tea can provide quick relief from a runny nose and help you feel better fast.

Hyptis suaveolens tea, also known as bush basil, is a natural remedy for runny nose. It contains compounds like eugenol and linalool, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. When you drink the tea, the anti-inflammatory compounds are absorbed into your body and can help to calm down the nasal tissues, reducing the production of excess mucus. This can provide quick relief from runny nose, congestion, and sinus pressure. The tea also has natural decongestant properties, which can help to clear out mucus and other debris that can clog up your sinuses, allowing for easier airflow and breathing. By using Hyptis suaveolens tea, you can naturally soothe your runny nose and start feeling better.

Lavandula angustifolia tea, also known as English lavender tea, is a herbal remedy that may help soothe a runny nose. The tea contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. The tea also has a decongestant effect, which can help relieve congestion and sinus pressure. When you drink English lavender tea, the anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties may help calm your nasal passages and reduce the production of excess mucus, making it a potential remedy for a runny nose.
